annotations.label: Mathematical model

anchor.snippet: an abstract description of a concrete system

annotations.note: Informal meta-mathematical notion of a model of a real-world system, not a formal object in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mathematical modeling

anchor.snippet: is termed mathematical modeling

annotations.note: Describes a human modeling activity, which is not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Linear vs. nonlinear model

anchor.snippet: the resulting mathematical model is defined as linear

annotations.note: An informal classification of models, not a defined predicate in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linear differential equation

anchor.snippet: a differential equation is said to be linear if it can be written with linear differential operators

annotations.note: Mathlib has ODE infrastructure but no dedicated definition of a linear differential equation; the 'linear differential' hits found are Kähler differentials, not ODEs.

annotations.label: Linear programming model

anchor.snippet: then the model is regarded as a linear model

annotations.note: No linear-programming framework or feasible-region/objective definition found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Static vs. dynamic model

anchor.snippet: A dynamic model accounts for time-dependent changes in the state of the system

annotations.note: Informal modeling taxonomy with no corresponding Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Explicit model

anchor.snippet: the model is said to be explicit

annotations.note: Informal description of solvability of model outputs, not a Mathlib object.

annotations.label: Implicit model

anchor.snippet: In such a case the model is said to be implicit

annotations.note: Informal notion; Mathlib's implicit function theorem is unrelated to this modeling classification.

annotations.label: Discrete vs. continuous model

anchor.snippet: A discrete model treats objects as discrete

annotations.note: Informal modeling taxonomy with no corresponding Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Deterministic model

anchor.snippet: A deterministic model is one in which every set of variable states is uniquely determined

annotations.note: Informal modeling notion, not a defined predicate in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stochastic model

anchor.snippet: randomness is present, and variable states are not described by unique values

annotations.note: Mathlib has probability theory and stochastic processes but no general definition of a 'stochastic model' as described.

annotations.label: Deductive model

anchor.snippet: A deductive model is a logical structure based on a theory

annotations.note: Epistemological classification of models, not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Inductive model

anchor.snippet: An inductive model arises from empirical findings and generalization from them

annotations.note: Epistemological classification of models, not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Floating model

anchor.snippet: it may be described as a 'floating' model

annotations.note: Epistemological classification of models, not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Strategic models

anchor.snippet: Strategic models assume that players are autonomous decision makers

annotations.note: Game-theoretic modeling concept; Mathlib has no general game/player framework formalizing this.

annotations.label: Model variables

anchor.snippet: decision variables , state variables , exogenous variables, and random variables

annotations.note: Informal taxonomy of variable roles in modeling, not a Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Black-box model

anchor.snippet: A black-box model is a system of which there is no a priori information available

annotations.note: Informal modeling notion with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: White-box model

anchor.snippet: A white-box model (also called glass box or clear box) is a system where all necessary information is available

annotations.note: Informal modeling notion with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Bent coin toss

anchor.snippet: a situation in which an experimenter bends a coin slightly and tosses it once

annotations.note: An illustrative scenario about subjective/Bayesian priors, not a formalized statement (despite Mathlib having Bernoulli PMFs).

annotations.label: Modeling aircraft flight

anchor.snippet: when modeling the flight of an aircraft

annotations.note: Physical modeling example, not a mathematical statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Overfitting

anchor.snippet: Statistical models are prone to overfitting

annotations.note: Statistical/ML concept with no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Training and tuning

anchor.snippet: the optimization of parameters is called training

annotations.note: Machine-learning parameter-fitting procedure, not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Interpolation and extrapolation

anchor.snippet: is called interpolation

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes polynomial (Lagrange) interpolation as a special case, but not the general modeling notion of interpolation vs. extrapolation within/outside a data range.

annotations.label: Scope of Newtonian mechanics

anchor.snippet: in evaluating Newtonian classical mechanics

annotations.note: Physical discussion of a model's domain of validity, not a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Computational models

anchor.snippet: Computational models are mathematical models used in computer simulations of physical systems

annotations.note: Describes simulation-based models, not a formalizable definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Deterministic finite automaton

anchor.snippet: the deterministic finite automaton (DFA) which is defined as an abstract mathematical concept

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `DFA` as a structure with states, alphabet, transition `step`, start state, and accept set, matching the standard definition.

annotations.label: Language recognized by the DFA

anchor.snippet: is the regular language given by the regular expression

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `DFA.accepts` and `RegularExpression.matches'` to express the language of a DFA and of a regex, but not the specific automaton/regex pairing of this example.

annotations.label: Map projection

anchor.snippet: A geographical map projection of a region of the earth onto a small, plane surface

annotations.note: Cartographic example, not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dead reckoning

anchor.snippet: predicting the position of a vehicle from its initial position, direction and speed of travel

annotations.note: Navigation example, not a formalized statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Population growth model

anchor.snippet: A simple (though approximate) model of population growth is the Malthusian growth model

annotations.note: No Malthusian/exponential population growth model is formalized in Mathlib (the exp-growth files found concern asymptotic growth rates, not this model).

annotations.label: Particle in a potential field

anchor.snippet: we consider a particle as being a point of mass which describes a trajectory in space

annotations.note: Classical mechanics example, not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: we consider a particle as being a point of mass which describes a trajectory in space

annotations.label: Neighbour-sensing model

anchor.snippet: Neighbour-sensing model is a model that explains the mushroom formation from the initially chaotic fungal network

annotations.note: Biological growth model, not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Simulating computer networks

anchor.snippet: mathematical models may be used to simulate computer networks

annotations.note: Application example, not a formalizable Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Rocket model in mechanics

anchor.snippet: mathematical models may be used to analyze the movement of a rocket model

annotations.note: Physics application example, not formalized in Mathlib.
